metadata.source: BPBD Kota Yogyakarta, PetaBencana.id, Tribun Jogja, Kompas, OpenStreetMap

hotspots.name: Jalan Kusumanegara
hotspots.description: Major east-west arterial road — low gradient causes waterlogging during heavy monsoon rainfall

hotspots.name: Jalan Gejayan
hotspots.description: University corridor road — heavy urbanization overwhelms drainage during rainstorms

hotspots.name: Jalan Ringroad Utara
hotspots.description: Northern ring road segment — underpass sections flood during intense rainfall

hotspots.name: Klitren
hotspots.description: High-risk neighborhood along Sungai Belik — BPBD auto-EWS station installed for flood warning

hotspots.name: Jalan Balirejo
hotspots.description: Road crossing Sungai Gajah Wong floodplain — periodic inundation from river overflow

hotspots.name: Jalan Ipda Tut Harsono
hotspots.description: Road in eastern Yogyakarta — drainage capacity insufficient for peak monsoon

hotspots.name: Terban
hotspots.description: Riverside kampung along Sungai Code — among most flood-affected neighborhoods in the city

hotspots.name: Bintaran
hotspots.description: Historic neighborhood near Sungai Code — recurring flood events during wet season

hotspots.name: Jalan Babarsari
hotspots.description: University area road in Sleman — rapid development outpaces storm drainage capacity

hotspots.name: Jalan Seturan
hotspots.description: Commercial road in Sleman — flat terrain and impervious surfaces trap rainwater

hotspots.name: Perumnas Seturan
hotspots.description: Housing complex in Sleman — low-lying area prone to waterlogging after heavy rain

hotspots.name: Selokan Mataram
hotspots.description: Road along historic irrigation canal — canal overflow floods surrounding areas during monsoon

hotspots.name: Jalan Affandi
hotspots.description: Major commercial road (formerly Gejayan) — intersection flooding blocks traffic during storms

hotspots.name: Jalan Urip Sumoharjo
hotspots.description: Central arterial road — waterlogging at multiple low points during heavy rainfall

hotspots.name: Jalan Laksda Adisucipto
hotspots.description: Airport road corridor — drainage overwhelmed during intense monsoon events

hotspots.name: Jalan Jendral Sudirman (Tugu Yogyakarta)
hotspots.description: Iconic boulevard from Tugu to Kraton — water accumulates at Tugu intersection during storms

hotspots.name: Jalan Majapahit
hotspots.description: Eastern road near Sungai Gajah Wong — bridge section floods during river surge events

hotspots.name: Gedongkuning - Wonocatur
hotspots.description: Neighborhood straddling city-Bantul border — low elevation near Sungai Gajah Wong floodplain

hotspots.name: Underpass Kentungan
hotspots.description: Ring Road underpass — critical flooding point, water reaches 50cm+ during heavy rain requiring road closure

hotspots.name: Gondokusuman (Sungai Code)
hotspots.description: Kelurahan along Sungai Code — recurrent flooding from river overflow, 8 EWS stations in corridor

hotspots.name: Jetis (Sungai Code)
hotspots.description: Dense urban area along Sungai Code banks — high flood risk from river surge

hotspots.name: Gedongtengen (Sungai Code)
hotspots.description: Old district along Sungai Code — narrow alleys impede evacuation during floods

hotspots.name: Cokrodiningratan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan at Sungai Code bend — river meander concentrates floodwater here

hotspots.name: Kota Baru (Sungai Code)
hotspots.description: Heritage district near Sungai Code — colonial-era drainage insufficient for monsoon

hotspots.name: Demangan
hotspots.description: Residential area along Sungai Code tributary — backwater flooding during heavy rain

hotspots.name: Kampung Code Utara
hotspots.description: Riverside settlement directly on Sungai Code banks — first impacted by rising water

hotspots.name: Sayidan
hotspots.description: Historic riverside community along Sungai Code — severe flooding events documented annually

hotspots.name: Prawirodirjan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan along Sungai Code southern stretch — flood risk from Code-Winongo confluence

hotspots.name: Tegalrejo (Sungai Winongo)
hotspots.description: Kecamatan along Sungai Winongo — 4 EWS stations monitor flood levels

hotspots.name: Ngampilan (Sungai Winongo)
hotspots.description: Low-lying area at Sungai Winongo floodplain — chronic inundation zone

hotspots.name: Mantrijeron
hotspots.description: Southern kecamatan along Sungai Winongo — flood risk amplified by urbanization

hotspots.name: Notoprajan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan beside Sungai Winongo — among highest-risk areas for riverine flooding

hotspots.name: Patangpuluhan
hotspots.description: Area near Sungai Winongo with recurring flood events during monsoon peaks

hotspots.name: Kricak (Sungai Winongo)
hotspots.description: Riverside kampung on Sungai Winongo — houses built close to river banks

hotspots.name: Wirobrajan
hotspots.description: Kecamatan between Winongo and Code rivers — dual flood risk from both corridors

hotspots.name: Umbulharjo (Sungai Gajah Wong)
hotspots.description: Largest kecamatan — Sungai Gajah Wong runs through center, 5 EWS stations

hotspots.name: Kotagede
hotspots.description: Heritage district along Sungai Gajah Wong — old drainage and narrow lanes

hotspots.name: Banguntapan
hotspots.description: Southern suburb along Sungai Gajah Wong — agricultural runoff compounds flood risk

hotspots.name: Muja Muju
hotspots.description: Kelurahan at Sungai Gajah Wong floodplain — water rises rapidly during storms

hotspots.name: Warungboto
hotspots.description: Kelurahan near Sungai Gajah Wong with recurrent waterlogging

hotspots.name: Giwangan
hotspots.description: Bus terminal area — low-lying terrain near Sungai Gajah Wong

hotspots.name: Iromejan (Klitren - Sungai Belik)
hotspots.description: Highest-risk flood zone in Yogyakarta — Sungai Belik auto-EWS station installed

hotspots.name: Baciro (Sungai Belik)
hotspots.description: Kelurahan along Sungai Belik — flash flood risk from rapid water rise

hotspots.name: Pakualaman (Sungai Tekik)
hotspots.description: Royal district along Sungai Tekik — auto-EWS station monitors flood levels

hotspots.name: Purwokinanti
hotspots.description: Kelurahan at Sungai Tekik corridor — periodic flooding during heavy monsoon

hotspots.name: Underpass Janti
hotspots.description: Major underpass on Ring Road — floods to 50cm+ during heavy rainfall events

hotspots.name: Underpass Monjali
hotspots.description: Northern ring road underpass — recurring waterlogging traps vehicles

hotspots.name: Jalan Godean
hotspots.description: Western arterial road — surface flooding during heavy rain events

hotspots.name: Jalan Kaliurang
hotspots.description: Northern arterial toward Merapi — volcanic lahar sediment clogs drainage

hotspots.name: Jalan Bantul
hotspots.description: Southern arterial road — low gradient collects runoff from surrounding areas

hotspots.name: Jalan Parangtritis
hotspots.description: Road to southern coast — crosses multiple river channels, floods at crossing points

hotspots.name: Jalan Solo (Adisucipto - Ring Road)
hotspots.description: Eastern arterial toward Solo — waterlogging at low points near airport area

hotspots.name: Mergangsan
hotspots.description: Kecamatan at Code-Winongo interfluve — drainage overwhelmed during intense rainfall

hotspots.name: Pandeyan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan in low-lying southern Umbulharjo — waterlogging from poor drainage

hotspots.name: Sorosutan
hotspots.description: Southern kelurahan with recurrent monsoon waterlogging

hotspots.name: Tahunan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan near Gajah Wong tributary — seasonal flooding from channel overflow

hotspots.name: Semaki
hotspots.description: Central kelurahan with drainage capacity issues during heavy rainfall

hotspots.name: Kadipaten
hotspots.description: Area near Kraton palace — historic drainage system inadequate for modern rainfall

hotspots.name: Purbayan (Kotagede)
hotspots.description: Heritage kelurahan in Kotagede — narrow lanes flood from Gajah Wong overflow

hotspots.name: Rejowinangun
hotspots.description: Eastern kelurahan near Sungai Gajah Wong — low-lying flood-prone terrain

hotspots.name: Caturtunggal (Depok)
hotspots.description: University area in Sleman — rapid urbanization outpaces drainage capacity

hotspots.name: Condongcatur
hotspots.description: Northern suburb — Selokan Mataram canal overflow during heavy monsoon rain

hotspots.name: Jalan Taman Siswa
hotspots.description: Central road near Sungai Code — waterlogging at low points during rain events

hotspots.name: Keparakan
hotspots.description: Kelurahan at Sungai Code floodplain — periodic inundation from river rise

hotspots.name: Brontokusuman
hotspots.description: Southern kelurahan near Code river — flood risk from combined river and surface water

hotspots.name: Jalan Imogiri Timur
hotspots.description: Eastern road crossing Sungai Gajah Wong — floods at river bridge section

hotspots.name: Panembahan
hotspots.description: Historic area near Kraton — old water channels overflow during heavy rain

hotspots.name: Ngupasan
hotspots.description: Commercial area at Malioboro south end — drainage issues during intense rainfall

hotspots.name: Prenggan (Kotagede)
hotspots.description: Kelurahan in Kotagede — historic silver district with poor modern drainage
